notes: This E2 run executes the SSRF-3432 pair as two separate IRIS M1 runs and archives them under one pair-level run root.
IRIS expects internal project slugs rather than the external case IDs, so the actual M1 invocations used bench__ssrf-ja-repo-cve-2023-3432_CVE-2023-3432_vuln/fixed.

Both M1 runs completed stage9 and produced empty source predicates in MySources.qll, which is closer to the original official baseline shape than the E1 report's reused DEEPSEEK_SELF01 self-baseline evidence.
M3 completed as a pair-level constrained JSON run and successfully recovered the LoadJson -> SURL -> fixed-guard differential with a parsed response on the first try.
M4 completed with the same context bundle but without a requested JSON schema, and the model still preserved the same target-aligned vulnerable/fixed differential explanation.
Because M4 already met the pair-level success criterion, M5 is not required for the current round and remains a reserve escalation path rather than a pending mandatory step.
Future pair-level results should be appended to the dedicated human report e2_ssrf3432_pair_differential_freedom_report.md instead of being merged into the cron-utils E2 report.
